fixtures.training_focus: Do not treat a visible type advantage as complete safety when a common early Rock threat is public-plausible.

public_notes: Geodude walls Flying damage but may threaten Rock damage.
Falkner should still feel like an early leader, not a mind reader.

actions.name: Gust
actions.explanation: Safe visible STAB into Geodude's resistance; likely low damage.
actions.public_tradeoff: Looks normal, but ignores the public Rock-risk prior.

actions.name: Sand Attack
actions.explanation: Reduces future Rock-risk without pretending to know the player's exact move.
actions.public_tradeoff: Annoying but fair if used sparingly.

actions.name: Switch to Noctowl
actions.explanation: Preserves Pidgeotto but does not solve Rock weakness cleanly.
actions.public_tradeoff: May be too passive for Falkner.

fixtures.training_focus: Teach when a boss should stop setting up because a publicly revealed threat already has tempo.

public_notes: Fire pressure is revealed, so Swords Dance has real risk.
Bugsy should still look bold, not suicidal.

actions.name: Wing Attack
actions.explanation: Cash damage before Quilava can punish Scyther.
actions.public_tradeoff: Straightforward tempo play.

actions.name: Quick Attack
actions.explanation: Priority chip if Scyther is trying to avoid a low-HP race.
actions.public_tradeoff: Weak if Quilava is not in range.

actions.name: Swords Dance
actions.explanation: Potentially scary if Quilava cannot punish, but Fire pressure is visible.
actions.public_tradeoff: May feel like a throw if Ember is obvious.

actions.name: Switch to Ledian
actions.explanation: Preserves Scyther but switches into the same Fire pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Probably too defensive and not useful.

fixtures.training_focus: Separate iconic pressure from bad Rollout locking when Rock/Ground danger is public.

public_notes: Rollout is Whitney-flavored but locks the ace.
Body Slam paralysis pressure is visible and explainable.

actions.name: Body Slam
actions.explanation: Keeps pressure without locking Miltank into a bad sequence.
actions.public_tradeoff: Less iconic but safer.

actions.name: Rollout
actions.explanation: Iconic snowball line, but Rock resistance and Magnitude risk are public.
actions.public_tradeoff: May be exciting or too robotic depending on taste.

actions.name: Milk Drink
actions.explanation: Preserves the ace while scouting whether Geodude has real damage.
actions.public_tradeoff: Can feel cheap if spammed at high HP.

actions.name: Switch to Girafarig
actions.explanation: Avoids Ground risk, but gives up Miltank's pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: A rare switch may feel smart; repeated switches may not.

fixtures.training_focus: Capture whether early utility moves should punish setup attempts or just take simple damage.

field.screens: player Reflect available but not active

public_notes: Encore can punish a support turn without hidden info.
Double Team can feel cheap if it is the default opener.

actions.name: Encore
actions.explanation: Targets a likely support pattern if Bayleef reveals it.
actions.public_tradeoff: Smart when timed; bad if blind-spammed.

actions.name: Thunder Wave
actions.explanation: Reliable speed control into a bulky starter.
actions.public_tradeoff: Strong, readable early utility.

actions.name: Double Team
actions.explanation: Raises evasion before the player has shown a punish.
actions.public_tradeoff: High risk of feeling cheap.

actions.name: DoubleSlap
actions.explanation: Simple chip that preserves the tutorial-like tone.
actions.public_tradeoff: May be too weak to feel prepared.

fixtures.training_focus: Decide when Destiny Bond is scary-good versus cheap or wasteful.

public_notes: Gengar is low enough that a sacrifice line is plausible.
The player can see Gengar's HP and plan around Destiny Bond once revealed.

actions.name: Shadow Ball
actions.explanation: Direct STAB pressure into a Psychic-type.
actions.public_tradeoff: Clean and explainable.

actions.name: Destiny Bond
actions.explanation: Punishes an obvious KO attempt while Gengar is low.
actions.public_tradeoff: Scary if occasional; cheap if overused.

actions.name: Spikes
actions.explanation: Long-game pressure that may waste a low-HP ace turn.
actions.public_tradeoff: Probably too slow here.

actions.name: Switch to Misdreavus
actions.explanation: Preserves Gengar, but risks another Ghost into Psychic pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Defensive but readable.

fixtures.training_focus: Label how often sleep should be the correct scary line instead of a cheap default.

public_notes: Sleep is part of Morty's identity.
The label should capture whether turn-2 Hypnosis feels fair.

actions.name: Hypnosis
actions.explanation: Status pressure into a bulky bird before it controls tempo.
actions.public_tradeoff: Leader-flavored but accuracy-dependent.

actions.name: Night Shade
actions.explanation: Reliable fixed damage without status swing.
actions.public_tradeoff: Fair but less threatening.

actions.name: Curse
actions.explanation: Ghost pressure at the cost of Haunter's HP.
actions.public_tradeoff: Can look dramatic or wasteful.

actions.name: Switch to Gengar
actions.explanation: Escalates to the ace early.
actions.public_tradeoff: May be too eager.

fixtures.training_focus: Teach the boss to value visible coverage without pretending it knows the player's next move.

public_notes: Ice Punch is legal on Poliwrath's set and cleanly targets Flying pressure.
Focus Punch is risky into a faster attacker.

actions.name: Ice Punch
actions.explanation: Coverage hit into the revealed Flying threat.
actions.public_tradeoff: Looks prepared without hidden information.

actions.name: Hypnosis
actions.explanation: Stops the bird if it lands.
actions.public_tradeoff: Swingy but leader-flavored.

actions.name: Focus Punch
actions.explanation: High reward if the player does not attack, but Wing Attack is visible.
actions.public_tradeoff: Can look foolish when the threat is obvious.

actions.name: Switch to Sudowoodo
actions.explanation: Hard walls Flying pressure but exposes a Water/Fighting team plan.
actions.public_tradeoff: Maybe smart if Chuck preserves Poliwrath.

fixtures.training_focus: Teach Jasmine to stay patient into reduced Fire chip while still respecting stronger public Fire coverage.

public_notes: Steelix is Steel/Dragon now; Imperial Scales makes neutral Fire damage less scary.
Revealed Flame Wheel is chip, not a reason to panic-switch.
Fire Blast is still a real public danger if the player spent the TM or voucher.
Rock Slide is super-effective into Fire in this hack; Earthquake is neutral but more accurate.

actions.name: Earthquake
actions.explanation: Accurate neutral hit into a Fire-type whose revealed Flame Wheel is not enough to force Steelix out.
actions.public_tradeoff: Reliable but much weaker than Rock Slide here.

actions.name: Rock Slide
actions.explanation: Super-effective Rock hit into Fire with real KO pressure at Quilava's current HP.
actions.public_tradeoff: Stronger damage line, but less accurate than Earthquake.

actions.name: Roar
actions.explanation: Rejects setup or switch patterns but gives up damage.
actions.public_tradeoff: Defensive Jasmine flavor.

actions.name: Switch to Skarmory
actions.explanation: Preserves Steelix but may overreact to reduced Fire chip.
actions.public_tradeoff: Likely too conservative unless Fire Blast risk dominates.

fixtures.training_focus: Label whether phazing a setup threat feels like good defense or slow stall.

public_notes: Machoke has shown setup intent.
Whirlwind is on Skarmory's real boss set.

actions.name: Whirlwind
actions.explanation: Deletes a setup line before it snowballs.
actions.public_tradeoff: Smart if setup is real; annoying if reflexive.

actions.name: Toxic
actions.explanation: Starts a clock on a bulky physical threat.
actions.public_tradeoff: Classic stall pressure.

actions.name: Steel Wing
actions.explanation: Direct damage without committing to stall.
actions.public_tradeoff: Probably low impact.

actions.name: Switch to Forretress
actions.explanation: A defensive pivot that may invite Fire pressure later.
actions.public_tradeoff: Team-plan move, not immediate punish.

fixtures.training_focus: Decide when Explosion is dramatic, fair, or cheap in a gym-leader fight.

public_notes: Cloyster is low, so trading may be rational.
Explosion can feel unfair if used as a surprise delete button too early.

actions.name: Surf
actions.explanation: Clean Water punish into Quilava.
actions.public_tradeoff: Strong and fair.

actions.name: Explosion
actions.explanation: Trades Cloyster for a major tempo swing.
actions.public_tradeoff: Needs taste labeling for cheapness.

actions.name: Protect
actions.explanation: Scouts and burns a turn at low HP.
actions.public_tradeoff: May be stally without hail or poison.

actions.name: Switch to Slowking
actions.explanation: Preserves Cloyster and moves into a bulky answer.
actions.public_tradeoff: Conservative Pryce line.

fixtures.training_focus: Teach when a Water/Psychic wall should respect Electric pressure and pivot to a Ground type.

public_notes: Piloswine is a public team member after prior reveal.
The right call may be a switch even if Slowking has status utility.

actions.name: Surf
actions.explanation: Neutral damage while staying in against Electric pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Simple but risky.

actions.name: Thunder Wave
actions.explanation: Speed control if Slowking can take the hit.
actions.public_tradeoff: Good utility if not too greedy.

actions.name: Rest
actions.explanation: Preserves Slowking if it expects a weaker turn.
actions.public_tradeoff: Likely bad into Electric pressure.

actions.name: Switch to Piloswine
actions.explanation: Ground pivot into a revealed Electric attack.
actions.public_tradeoff: Prepared and fair if based on public typing.

fixtures.training_focus: The Suicune/Ice Beam test: the AI should respect public meta coverage before it is revealed.

public_notes: Dragonair resists Surf but is weak to Ice.
The AI must not know the exact hidden moveset; it can use public priors.

actions.name: Thunder
actions.explanation: Punishes Water typing immediately if Dragonair stays in.
actions.public_tradeoff: Strong but risks hidden Ice coverage.

actions.name: Outrage
actions.explanation: Commits Dragonair into a lock while Ice coverage is plausible.
actions.public_tradeoff: May be too reckless.

actions.name: Switch to Kingdra
actions.explanation: Pivots into Clair's ace and handles Water/Ice pressure better.
actions.public_tradeoff: Smart if Clair values Kingdra as her ace.

actions.name: Switch to Mantine
actions.explanation: Water-resistant pivot with different risks.
actions.public_tradeoff: Defensive but public-info legal.

fixtures.training_focus: Label when Clair should spend a turn on Dragon Dance instead of taking damage now.

public_notes: Kingdra is not in obvious immediate KO range.
Dragon Dance can convert the endgame if Lapras cannot punish enough.

actions.name: Dragon Dance
actions.explanation: Sets up a sweep if Kingdra survives the next hit.
actions.public_tradeoff: Scary-good or greedy depending on HP taste.

actions.name: Surf
actions.explanation: Reliable chip in a Water mirror.
actions.public_tradeoff: Low drama but safe.

actions.name: Outrage
actions.explanation: Commits to damage and lock-in.
actions.public_tradeoff: Aggressive Clair line.

actions.name: Ice Beam
actions.explanation: Neutral coverage with freeze chance.
actions.public_tradeoff: May be the boring safe line.

fixtures.training_focus: Capture Koga's poison-clock identity without ignoring immediate Psychic pressure.

public_notes: Crobat may outspeed and threaten a KO.
Toxic may be too slow into a dangerous Psychic-type.

actions.name: Wing Attack
actions.explanation: Immediate damage before Alakazam can Recover or attack.
actions.public_tradeoff: Tempo-first Koga line.

actions.name: Toxic
actions.explanation: Starts the poison clock.
actions.public_tradeoff: Very Koga, but maybe greedy.

actions.name: Confuse Ray
actions.explanation: Disruption into a dangerous attacker.
actions.public_tradeoff: Swingy and potentially annoying.

actions.name: Switch to Umbreon
actions.explanation: Publicly strong Psychic answer if Umbreon is known.
actions.public_tradeoff: Smart preservation line.

fixtures.training_focus: Lance's current Dragon-heavy roster decides whether Dragonite should set up, chip, or pivot around public Ice coverage.

public_notes: Champion Dragonite is a very high-value mon.
The current Lance bench can pivot through Kingdra, Gyarados, Steelix, Ampharos, or Yanma, but all have different public coverage risks.
A smart champion can respect public Ice coverage without reading the hidden move slot.

actions.name: Dragon Dance
actions.explanation: Greedy setup into a Pokemon likely to carry Ice coverage.
actions.public_tradeoff: Strong if Suicune lacks Ice Beam; catastrophic if it has it.

actions.name: Outrage
actions.explanation: Commits to immediate chip and probes whether Suicune stays for Ice coverage.
actions.public_tradeoff: Damage plus information, but locks Dragonite.

actions.name: Earthquake
actions.explanation: Coverage that avoids Outrage lock but is weaker into Suicune.
actions.public_tradeoff: Keeps options open at the cost of pressure.

actions.name: Switch to Kingdra
actions.explanation: Preserves Dragonite and handles Water/Ice pressure better than most dragons.
actions.public_tradeoff: Strong preservation line if Lance values the ace.

actions.name: Switch to Gyarados
actions.explanation: Water-resistant pivot that still fears Electric coverage.
actions.public_tradeoff: Useful scout if Electric risk is low.

actions.name: Switch to Steelix
actions.explanation: Preserves Dragonite while resisting some neutral chip, but hates Surf.
actions.public_tradeoff: Bad if Suicune simply attacks with Water.

fixtures.training_focus: Label whether a late-game Rock leader should trade Golem or preserve it under Water pressure.

public_notes: Surf pressure is revealed.
Explosion is legal and visible only after it happens, so taste matters.

actions.name: Earthquake
actions.explanation: Best normal damage while accepting Water risk.
actions.public_tradeoff: Straightforward but maybe not enough.

actions.name: Explosion
actions.explanation: Trades Golem to stop Vaporeon from sweeping.
actions.public_tradeoff: Can be dramatic or cheap.

actions.name: Curse
actions.explanation: Setup while under direct Water pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Likely too greedy.

actions.name: Switch to Omastar
actions.explanation: Preserves Golem and resists Water better.
actions.public_tradeoff: Defensive but sensible.

fixtures.training_focus: Decide when a fast ace should Recover versus press coverage into a Grass threat.

public_notes: Recover is strong but can become robotic.
Psychic damage through Reflect may be the tempo play.

actions.name: Recover
actions.explanation: Preserves Starmie while rain remains active.
actions.public_tradeoff: Correct at low HP; annoying if automatic.

actions.name: Psychic
actions.explanation: Presses damage into Meganium without relying on Water.
actions.public_tradeoff: Tempo-focused ace play.

actions.name: Hydro Pump
actions.explanation: Rain-boosted STAB into a Grass resist.
actions.public_tradeoff: Big but likely inefficient.

actions.name: Switch to Lapras
actions.explanation: Bulky pivot with Ice Beam pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Smart if preserving Starmie is the priority.

fixtures.training_focus: Label Erika's grass-control style against a bulky normal wall.

public_notes: Snorlax is a wall that may Rest off chip.
Sleep Powder and Swords Dance both fit Erika differently.

actions.name: Sleep Powder
actions.explanation: Controls the wall before it can Rest or Curse.
actions.public_tradeoff: Strong status line; taste decides cheapness.

actions.name: Swords Dance
actions.explanation: Attempts to break through with Muscle Band pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Scary if Snorlax cannot punish.

actions.name: Sludge Bomb
actions.explanation: Immediate strong neutral hit.
actions.public_tradeoff: Simple pressure.

actions.name: Switch to Exeggutor
actions.explanation: Special pressure and Explosion threat later.
actions.public_tradeoff: May reveal too much too early.

fixtures.training_focus: Teach a late-game ace not to autopick Psychic into a public Dark answer.

public_notes: Psychic may fail into Dark typing.
Choice Specs makes the first pick especially important.

actions.name: Psychic
actions.explanation: Highest identity move but bad into a public Dark type.
actions.public_tradeoff: Too robotic if chosen blindly.

actions.name: ThunderPunch
actions.explanation: Neutral coverage without locking into a failed Psychic.
actions.public_tradeoff: Readable adaptation.

actions.name: Ice Punch
actions.explanation: Neutral coverage with freeze chance.
actions.public_tradeoff: Safe but lower identity.

actions.name: Switch to Hypno
actions.explanation: Bulky status pivot away from Dark pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Defensive champion-level play.

fixtures.training_focus: Blue should feel adaptable: punish the visible threat, not just use the biggest STAB.

public_notes: Arcanine has Crunch on its real set.
Blue should punish known team shape without impossible reads.

actions.name: Crunch
actions.explanation: Coverage into Starmie that respects Water resistance to Fire.
actions.public_tradeoff: Prepared final-boss behavior.

actions.name: ExtremeSpeed
actions.explanation: Priority is useful only if Starmie is near KO.
actions.public_tradeoff: Premature at 61% HP.

actions.name: Flamethrower
actions.explanation: Strong identity move into a resist.
actions.public_tradeoff: Too robotic if selected by STAB habit.

actions.name: Switch to Porygon2
actions.explanation: Bulky coverage mirror with Recover.
actions.public_tradeoff: Defensive but plausible.

fixtures.training_focus: First-leader Pidgeotto should weigh one accuracy debuff against honest chip when Fire pressure is public.

public_notes: Pidgeotto is Falkner's real opener in the current trainer source.
Charmander has revealed Ember, but this is still an early-leader teaching spot.

actions.name: Tackle
actions.explanation: Plain chip into a Fire starter.
actions.public_tradeoff: Readable but low pressure.

actions.name: Sand Attack
actions.explanation: One accuracy drop can reduce future Ember risk.
actions.public_tradeoff: Useful once; repeated debuffs can become passive.

actions.name: Gust
actions.explanation: Flying STAB chip from the real Pidgeotto set.
actions.public_tradeoff: Honest pressure, but not a clean answer.

actions.name: Switch to Noctowl
actions.explanation: Preserves Pidgeotto and uses the bulkier bird.
actions.public_tradeoff: Too much escalation if the opener is still healthy.

fixtures.training_focus: Should Falkner finish a weakened Pikachu with direct pressure or spend a turn reducing Electric risk?

public_notes: Pikachu paralyzed Pidgeotto last turn (revealed).
Quick Attack can finish low HP targets if damage is enough; Sand Attack is slower but reduces Electric pressure.

actions.name: Gust
actions.explanation: Best STAB chip from the current Pidgeotto set.
actions.public_tradeoff: Straightforward pressure.

actions.name: Quick Attack
actions.explanation: Priority chip if Pikachu is near KO.
actions.public_tradeoff: Weak if Pikachu is not in range.

actions.name: Sand Attack
actions.explanation: Accuracy control before more Electric chip lands.
actions.public_tradeoff: Can be too passive into a weakened target.

actions.name: Switch to Noctowl
actions.explanation: Preserves Pidgeotto for later.
actions.public_tradeoff: Gives up tempo against a weakened Pikachu.

fixtures.training_focus: Confirm setup is correct when the visible matchup actually allows it (mirror to the unsafe-SD fixture).

public_notes: Scyther outspeeds Geodude.
Geodude's revealed damage cannot threaten Scyther this turn.
Even Magnitude needs an awkward roll to cleanly KO Scyther.

actions.name: Wing Attack
actions.explanation: Direct STAB into a Rock-resist; minimal damage.
actions.public_tradeoff: Wastes a setup window.

actions.name: Swords Dance
actions.explanation: Doubles Atk before Geodude can punish.
actions.public_tradeoff: Should read as scary-good here.

actions.name: Quick Attack
actions.explanation: Priority chip while ahead in HP.
actions.public_tradeoff: Surrenders the free SD turn.

actions.name: Switch to Ariados
actions.explanation: Hazard pivot off the ace.
actions.public_tradeoff: Burns Scyther's safe setup.

fixtures.training_focus: Bugsy's opener should decide between Toxic pressure, weak chip, and preserving Ariados when a Flying threat is public.

public_notes: Ariados is Bugsy's real opener in the current trainer source.
Pidgey has public Flying pressure, so status has to be weighed against taking tempo loss.
Scyther is still available as the ace if Ariados should preserve HP.

actions.name: Toxic
actions.explanation: Starts poison pressure before Pidgey can chip Ariados or pivot out.
actions.public_tradeoff: Status tempo can be correct, but it gives Pidgey a free attack if damage matters now.

actions.name: Poison Sting
actions.explanation: Small chip with a poison chance while avoiding a full status turn.
actions.public_tradeoff: Low impact unless poison lands.

actions.name: Giga Drain
actions.explanation: Sustain damage that keeps Ariados healthy if the matchup is not immediately dangerous.
actions.public_tradeoff: Neutral into Pidgey in this hack, but better if the player pivots to a Rock or Ground answer.

actions.name: Leech Life
actions.explanation: Uses Ariados's Bug STAB for direct damage and recovery.
actions.public_tradeoff: Resisted into Pidgey, so it may read as low-tempo.

actions.name: Switch to Scyther
actions.explanation: Preserves Ariados and brings in Bugsy's ace against the public Flying matchup.
actions.public_tradeoff: Aggressive pivot, but it exposes Scyther early.

fixtures.training_focus: Attract is iconic but only legal-information correct against a male. Capture when it is bold play vs cheap.

public_notes: Machop's gender is publicly known once it appears (sprite/gender symbol).
Assume male here.
Attract halves opposing turn output but sets up nothing on its own.

actions.name: Body Slam
actions.explanation: Best STAB damage and paralysis chance.
actions.public_tradeoff: Clean and explainable.

actions.name: Attract
actions.explanation: Disables half of Machop's turns; iconic Whitney move.
actions.public_tradeoff: Can feel cheap if reflexive.

actions.name: Rollout
actions.explanation: Snowball if uncontested; weak first turn.
actions.public_tradeoff: Locks the ace; bad against Fighting STAB.

actions.name: Stomp
actions.explanation: Less damage than Body Slam but flinch chance.
actions.public_tradeoff: Fine but strictly worse than Body Slam given Body Slam is also revealed.

fixtures.training_focus: Capture whether Perish Song reads as scary chess or stall when the player is set up.

public_notes: Typhlosion has +1 Atk from a previous Curse-style trade.
Perish Song forces a switch race within 3 turns.

actions.name: Perish Song
actions.explanation: Forces Typhlosion off in 3 turns; resets the boost.
actions.public_tradeoff: Smart at endgame; cheap if used reflexively early.

actions.name: Confuse Ray
actions.explanation: Random damage at the cost of self-hits.
actions.public_tradeoff: Already revealed and underwhelming alone.

actions.name: Psywave
actions.explanation: Variable damage; rarely closes a kill.
actions.public_tradeoff: Filler when better lines exist.

actions.name: Switch to Gengar
actions.explanation: Pivots to the ace into Fire pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Throws Misdreavus into Fire chip first.

fixtures.training_focus: Chuck should decide whether to press Fighting STAB, cover a switch, or preserve Hitmonlee when a Fighting mirror is public.

public_notes: Machoke used Bulk Up turn 1 and Karate Chop turn 2 (revealed).
Hitmonlee can attack immediately, but Sudowoodo is available as a physical resist pivot.

actions.name: Hi Jump Kick
actions.explanation: Strong Fighting STAB into a Fighting mirror.
actions.public_tradeoff: High reward, but crash risk matters if blocked or missed.

actions.name: Rock Slide
actions.explanation: Coverage that avoids Hi Jump Kick crash risk.
actions.public_tradeoff: Lower direct payoff into Machoke.

actions.name: Foresight
actions.explanation: Utility from the real Hitmonlee set.
actions.public_tradeoff: Too slow unless a Ghost pivot is public.

actions.name: Switch to Sudowoodo
actions.explanation: Preserves Hitmonlee and brings in a physical wall.
actions.public_tradeoff: Defensive pivot that gives up immediate damage.

fixtures.training_focus: Chuck should weigh sleep, coverage, and preservation when Poliwrath faces a faster Psychic threat.

public_notes: Alakazam outspeeds Poliwrath.
Hypnosis can neutralize the Psychic threat if it lands; Ice Punch is the safer direct line from the real set.

actions.name: Surf
actions.explanation: Reliable STAB chip.
actions.public_tradeoff: Simple but not enough into a fast Psychic attacker.

actions.name: Ice Punch
actions.explanation: Coverage damage with freeze chance.
actions.public_tradeoff: Lower risk than sleep but may not swing tempo enough.

actions.name: Hypnosis
actions.explanation: Sleep can stop Alakazam before it snowballs.
actions.public_tradeoff: Accuracy-dependent and must respect Sleep Clause.

actions.name: Switch to Umbreon
actions.explanation: Preserves Poliwrath and uses the Dark-type pivot.
actions.public_tradeoff: Smart if Umbreon is healthy; passive if overused.

fixtures.training_focus: Is Thunder Wave correct when the user is faster, or is direct Thunder damage strictly better?

public_notes: Magneton is slower than Quilava.
Steelix in the back does not resist Fire or Ground; it can threaten back with Earthquake if it survives the pivot.
Thunder Wave halves Quilava's speed and lets Magneton outspeed.

actions.name: Thunder Wave
actions.explanation: Flips the speed tier and enables follow-up damage.
actions.public_tradeoff: Trades a turn of damage for tempo.

actions.name: Thunderbolt
actions.explanation: Direct damage now.
actions.public_tradeoff: Quilava is faster; this is a one-shot trade.

actions.name: Lock-On
actions.explanation: Combos with Zap Cannon next turn.
actions.public_tradeoff: Two-turn setup under fire pressure.

actions.name: Switch to Steelix
actions.explanation: Pivots to a bulkier ace that threatens Earthquake back.
actions.public_tradeoff: Burns Magneton's role too early.

fixtures.training_focus: Decide whether to set up SpD against a Fire opponent or just race the kill.

public_priors: Earthquake plausible if TM was spent

public_notes: Flamethrower is revealed; Piloswine is 2x weak to Fire.
Amnesia raises SpD to make Flamethrower survivable, but two turns of damage may already be lethal.

actions.name: Earthquake
actions.explanation: Best damage line right now.
actions.public_tradeoff: Race; may not KO before the second Flamethrower.

actions.name: Amnesia
actions.explanation: Doubles SpD to survive multiple Flamethrowers.
actions.public_tradeoff: Setup under direct super-effective damage; high risk.

actions.name: Take Down
actions.explanation: Strong physical hit with recoil.
actions.public_tradeoff: Less than Earthquake; recoil compounds.

actions.name: Switch to Cloyster
actions.explanation: Cloyster also dislikes Fire but can hazard-stack.
actions.public_tradeoff: Awkward pivot; Cloyster takes Fire neutrally but enters already damaged.

fixtures.training_focus: Mirror match: when does the boss DD before the player DDs vs preempt with damage?

public_notes: Kingdra is Clair's current ace and has Dragon Dance, Surf, Outrage, and Ice Beam.
Outrage is publicly Dragon-only in this hack; Dragonair is a real Dragon.
Kingdra outspeeds non-DD Dragonair; player Dragon Dance can flip that race.

actions.name: Dragon Dance
actions.explanation: Boosts Kingdra before the mirror gets out of hand.
actions.public_tradeoff: Strong if Dragonair cannot punish immediately.

actions.name: Surf
actions.explanation: Reliable STAB chip.
actions.public_tradeoff: Low-risk but may be too slow in a Dragon mirror.

actions.name: Outrage
actions.explanation: Direct Dragon pressure into Dragonair.
actions.public_tradeoff: Locks Kingdra if the player pivots.

actions.name: Ice Beam
actions.explanation: Neutral coverage with freeze chance.
actions.public_tradeoff: Safe but lower drama than Dragon pressure.

actions.name: Switch to Steelix
actions.explanation: Uses Steelix to absorb Dragon pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Gives up ace tempo.

fixtures.training_focus: Clair's Dragonair should choose between immediate Thunder damage and speed control into an Electric threat.

public_notes: Dragonair has Thunder, Fire Blast, Outrage, and Thunder Wave in the current source.
Ampharos threatens Electric damage, but Dragonair can either attack or slow it down.

actions.name: Thunder
actions.explanation: High-power coverage into Ampharos.
actions.public_tradeoff: Accuracy risk, but the damage swing is real.

actions.name: Thunder Wave
actions.explanation: Speed control before Ampharos can keep pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Can be too slow if Thunder is already enough.

actions.name: Outrage
actions.explanation: Reliable Dragon damage without Thunder accuracy risk.
actions.public_tradeoff: Lock-in can be punished by a switch.

actions.name: Switch to Steelix
actions.explanation: Steelix resists Electric pressure and threatens Earthquake.
actions.public_tradeoff: Conservative pivot that reveals the answer.

fixtures.training_focus: Will's current Xatu should choose immediate Psychic pressure, delayed Future Sight, or a Fire-resistant pivot.

public_notes: Xatu has Night Shade, Psychic, Future Sight, and Drill Peck in the current source.
Flamethrower pressure is public, so delayed setup has to justify the free hit.

actions.name: Psychic
actions.explanation: Strong direct STAB into Typhlosion.
actions.public_tradeoff: Clean pressure from the current set.

actions.name: Future Sight
actions.explanation: Delayed Psychic pressure that can pair with a pivot.
actions.public_tradeoff: Too slow if Flamethrower immediately wins tempo.

actions.name: Drill Peck
actions.explanation: Physical Flying coverage from Xatu's current set.
actions.public_tradeoff: Lower damage but avoids special-only predictability.

actions.name: Switch to Slowbro
actions.explanation: Slowbro resists Fire pressure better than Xatu.
actions.public_tradeoff: Gives up Will's opener tempo.

fixtures.training_focus: Setup against a fast Dark/Crunch threat is a tempo trap even though Amnesia boosts the correct defensive stat.

public_notes: Dark is special in this hack, matching Gen 2 type-category rules.
Slowbro Amnesia raises SpD, so it helps against Crunch but costs a turn into a faster threat.
Houndoom is a public counter-pressure point, so direct Surf or a Dark-resistant pivot must be weighed carefully.

actions.name: Amnesia
actions.explanation: Raises Slowbro's special bulk.
actions.public_tradeoff: Correct defensive axis, but spending the turn may still lose tempo into fast Crunch.

actions.name: Surf
actions.explanation: Super-effective STAB into Houndoom.
actions.public_tradeoff: Cleanest direct answer if Slowbro stays in.

actions.name: Psychic
actions.explanation: Secondary STAB from Slowbro's current set.
actions.public_tradeoff: Worse than Surf into Houndoom.

actions.name: Switch to Houndoom
actions.explanation: Preserves Slowbro and resists Houndoom's Dark/Fire pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Conservative mirror pivot that gives up immediate Surf pressure.

fixtures.training_focus: Will's Alakazam should choose clean Psychic damage or coverage when a Fighting threat is active.

public_notes: Alakazam outspeeds Machamp.
Psychic is the clean STAB line; elemental punches cover switches but lose immediate pressure.

actions.name: Psychic
actions.explanation: Direct super-effective STAB into Machamp.
actions.public_tradeoff: Clean and explainable.

actions.name: Ice Punch
actions.explanation: Coverage that can catch Flying or Ground pivots.
actions.public_tradeoff: Lower damage into Machamp.

actions.name: ThunderPunch
actions.explanation: Coverage into Water or Flying pivots.
actions.public_tradeoff: Wrong target if Machamp stays in.

actions.name: Fire Punch
actions.explanation: Coverage into Steel or Bug pivots.
actions.public_tradeoff: Lower immediate value than Psychic.

actions.name: Switch to Slowbro
actions.explanation: Preserves Alakazam and uses a bulkier Psychic type.
actions.public_tradeoff: Conservative when Alakazam has tempo.

fixtures.training_focus: Koga's current Ariados lead should choose hazards, poison pressure, or a Fire-resistant pivot under revealed Fire pressure.

public_notes: Ariados is Koga's current hazard lead.
Flamethrower is revealed, so a full hazard turn has real tempo risk.

actions.name: Spikes
actions.explanation: Sets a long-game hazard from Ariados's real set.
actions.public_tradeoff: Strong team-plan move if Ariados can afford the Fire hit.

actions.name: Toxic
actions.explanation: Starts Koga's poison clock.
actions.public_tradeoff: Useful, but slower than escaping Fire pressure.

actions.name: Leech Life
actions.explanation: Direct Bug chip with sustain.
actions.public_tradeoff: Resisted and low impact into Typhlosion.

actions.name: Switch to Tentacruel
actions.explanation: Preserves Ariados and uses a Fire-resistant Water/Poison pivot.
actions.public_tradeoff: Good defensive fit but gives up hazard tempo.

fixtures.training_focus: Janine's Qwilfish should finish the third Spikes layer when the player is grounded, no spin has been revealed, and no immediate KO threat is public.

public_notes: Local source supports three Spikes layers at one-eighth, one-sixth, and one-quarter max HP.
Two layers are already on the player side, so another Spikes turn changes every grounded switch from one-sixth to one-quarter max HP.
No Rapid Spin user has been revealed on the player side.
Snorlax is not showing an immediate KO route into Qwilfish.

actions.name: Spikes
actions.explanation: Places the third local Spikes layer.
actions.public_tradeoff: Converts future grounded switches and Rest cycles into much higher pressure.

actions.name: Sludge Bomb
actions.explanation: Direct poison STAB into Snorlax.
actions.public_tradeoff: Safe chip, but it does not change the hazard economy.

actions.name: Surf
actions.explanation: Neutral Water coverage.
actions.public_tradeoff: No immediate KO is public.

actions.name: Explosion
actions.explanation: Sacrifices Qwilfish for tempo.
actions.public_tradeoff: Too early while Qwilfish can still finish the hazard stack.

actions.name: Switch to Tentacruel
actions.explanation: Preserves Qwilfish and brings in Janine's spinner.
actions.public_tradeoff: Gives up the open third-layer turn.

fixtures.training_focus: When three Spikes layers already exist, Janine's Qwilfish must stop clicking Spikes and use a live move.

public_notes: Local Spikes placement fails once three layers are already on that side.
The hazard route is already established; Qwilfish needs to use a live move or preserve itself.

actions.name: Spikes
actions.explanation: Attempts a fourth local Spikes layer.
actions.public_tradeoff: Fails once three layers are already set.

actions.name: Sludge Bomb
actions.explanation: Direct poison STAB into Snorlax.
actions.public_tradeoff: A live attack after the hazard stack is complete.

actions.name: Surf
actions.explanation: Neutral Water coverage.
actions.public_tradeoff: A live move, though less aligned with Qwilfish's Poison pressure.

actions.name: Explosion
actions.explanation: Sacrifices Qwilfish for material.
actions.public_tradeoff: Can be held until the completed hazard stack creates a better use.

actions.name: Switch to Tentacruel
actions.explanation: Preserves Qwilfish and brings in Janine's spinner.
actions.public_tradeoff: Conservative, but at least avoids a failed Spikes click.

fixtures.training_focus: Spider Web prevents switching but gives up damage; capture when trap is correct.

public_notes: Ariados has Spider Web, Toxic, Spikes, and Leech Life in the current source.
Quilava can threaten Fire damage, so trapping has to beat immediate tempo loss.

actions.name: Spider Web
actions.explanation: Traps Quilava for a follow-up Toxic line.
actions.public_tradeoff: Only pays off if Ariados survives the Fire pressure.

actions.name: Leech Life
actions.explanation: Direct Bug chip with sustain.
actions.public_tradeoff: Resisted and low impact into Quilava.

actions.name: Toxic
actions.explanation: Starts poison pressure after the trap idea.
actions.public_tradeoff: Can be too slow into Fire damage.

actions.name: Switch to Crobat
actions.explanation: Preserves Ariados and brings in Koga's ace.
actions.public_tradeoff: Still has to respect Fire and Electric coverage.

fixtures.training_focus: Two strong damage options with different accuracy/spread tradeoffs into a faster Psychic threat.

public_notes: Alakazam outspeeds Machamp by a large margin.
Cross Chop has 80% accuracy and high crit; Rock Slide is 90% with flinch chance.

actions.name: Cross Chop
actions.explanation: Best damage if it lands.
actions.public_tradeoff: 80% accuracy; whiffing into Psychic loses the round.

actions.name: Rock Slide
actions.explanation: More reliable damage with flinch chance.
actions.public_tradeoff: Lower damage but better expected value.

actions.name: Foresight
actions.explanation: Removes Ghost immunity; Alakazam isn't Ghost.
actions.public_tradeoff: Wrong tool here.

actions.name: Vital Throw
actions.explanation: Negative-priority STAB, never misses.
actions.public_tradeoff: Way less damage; only correct if Cross Chop accuracy is the deciding fear.

fixtures.training_focus: Big damage with crash recoil vs safe damage when the matchup is otherwise winning.

public_notes: Hi Jump Kick crashes for 50% of Hitmonlee's HP if it misses or is blocked.
Whirlwind would phaze Hitmonlee out and reset HJK reads.
Brick Break breaks screens and never crashes.

actions.name: Hi Jump Kick
actions.explanation: Highest damage available; crashes if Whirlwind triggers or it misses.
actions.public_tradeoff: All-or-nothing; bold.

actions.name: Brick Break
actions.explanation: Reliable damage; no recoil.
actions.public_tradeoff: Less damage but never throws.

actions.name: Swagger
actions.explanation: Boosts Skarmory's Atk + confusion.
actions.public_tradeoff: Skarmory's Atk is already terrifying with Drill Peck.

actions.name: Switch to Machamp
actions.explanation: Bulkier into Drill Peck risk.
actions.public_tradeoff: Loses Hitmonlee's speed advantage.

fixtures.training_focus: Sandstorm gives long-game chip but Onix at low HP could trade. Capture taste for slow vs fast finish.

public_notes: Earthquake KOs Onix at 31%.
Sandstorm chips Typhlosion every turn but only if the field stays Onix-up.
Explosion KOs Typhlosion outright.

actions.name: Sandstorm
actions.explanation: Long-game chip if Onix survives.
actions.public_tradeoff: Onix dies first; sand stays but the chip lands on Typhlosion only if it's still in.

actions.name: Rock Slide
actions.explanation: Best non-trade damage.
actions.public_tradeoff: Slow under super-effective return fire.

actions.name: Explosion
actions.explanation: Trades Onix for Typhlosion.
actions.public_tradeoff: Tempo swing if Lapras is the player's only follow-up.

actions.name: Switch to Machamp
actions.explanation: Bulky pivot into Earthquake risk.
actions.public_tradeoff: Wastes Onix's last turn entirely.

fixtures.training_focus: Pursuit pays off only if the player switches; Flamethrower is the safe direct line. Capture when prediction is fair.

public_notes: Alakazam may switch out of Houndoom pressure.
Pursuit doubles damage on switching targets; Flamethrower is the safe direct line if it stays.

actions.name: Pursuit
actions.explanation: Doubled damage if Alakazam switches; weak otherwise.
actions.public_tradeoff: Smart if the switch read is real.

actions.name: Flamethrower
actions.explanation: Strong damage either way.
actions.public_tradeoff: Safer baseline.

actions.name: Crunch
actions.explanation: Direct STAB Dark hit.
actions.public_tradeoff: Same as Flamethrower-tier baseline.

actions.name: Switch to Murkrow
actions.explanation: Faster Pursuit user; loses Houndoom's lead role.
actions.public_tradeoff: Awkward turn-1 burn.

fixtures.training_focus: Karen's current Crobat should decide between Toxic clock, direct Flying damage, and a Tyranitar pivot into Dragonite.

public_notes: Outrage is publicly Dragon-only; Dragonite locks for 2-3 turns.
Toxic creates Karen-style long-game pressure, while Tyranitar is a cleaner anti-Dragon pivot.

actions.name: Toxic
actions.explanation: Times out Dragonite while Outrage burns turns.
actions.public_tradeoff: Clean long-term clock if Crobat survives.

actions.name: Wing Attack
actions.explanation: Direct Flying chip from Crobat's current set.
actions.public_tradeoff: Honest damage, but Dragonite can be bulky.

actions.name: Confuse Ray
actions.explanation: Disruption into a locked or boosting Dragonite.
actions.public_tradeoff: Swingy and can feel annoying.

actions.name: Switch to Tyranitar
actions.explanation: Uses Karen's Rock/Dark bruiser into Dragon pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Strong pivot if it avoids taking too much chip.

fixtures.training_focus: Destiny Bond on the last leader's last mon: scary-good gauntlet seal, or cheap reflex?

public_notes: Karen has no bench; Gengar is the last mon.
Destiny Bond + a faster Snorlax KO trades both.
Thunderbolt is weak into Snorlax (high SpD).

actions.name: Destiny Bond
actions.explanation: Trades both mons; ends the run if Snorlax was Gengar's only answer.
actions.public_tradeoff: Endgame DB is rated scary-good in design.

actions.name: Thunderbolt
actions.explanation: Direct damage but Snorlax is bulky on the special side.
actions.public_tradeoff: Probably too slow.

actions.name: Shadow Ball
actions.explanation: Less effective into Normal types.
actions.public_tradeoff: Wrong move in this matchup.

actions.name: Curse
actions.explanation: Paranoid setup; halves Gengar HP.
actions.public_tradeoff: Wastes the last turn.

fixtures.training_focus: Current Lance Dragonite no longer has Thunder or Fire Blast; compare immediate Outrage pressure against greedy Dragon Dance and preservation pivots.

public_priors: Ice Fang unavailable in this ruleset

public_notes: Aerodactyl outspeeds Dragonite slightly.
Earthquake cannot hit a Flying target.
Outrage is the clean legal pressure move but creates lock-in risk.

actions.name: Outrage
actions.explanation: Best direct legal pressure into Aerodactyl.
actions.public_tradeoff: Locks Dragonite while Vaporeon and Zapdos are seen.

actions.name: Dragon Dance
actions.explanation: Tries to reverse the speed problem before attacking.
actions.public_tradeoff: Greedy under revealed Rock Slide pressure.

actions.name: Hyper Beam
actions.explanation: Big neutral hit with recharge liability.
actions.public_tradeoff: Bad if it does not KO.

actions.name: Switch to Steelix
actions.explanation: Defensive pivot into Rock pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: May invite Vaporeon for free.

actions.name: Switch to Kingdra
actions.explanation: Preserves the ace and avoids Flying immunity problems.
actions.public_tradeoff: Gives up immediate pressure.

fixtures.training_focus: Ampharos is now part of Lance's Dragon roster; teach when Dragon Dance is worth it versus immediate Thunder pressure.

public_notes: Thunder is inaccurate but heavily boosted by Magnet.
Dragon Dance uses the smart-attack mechanic on special-leaning dragons.
Recover means weak chip can be erased if Ampharos gets too cute.

actions.name: Thunder
actions.explanation: Immediate boosted damage before Porygon2 can Recover loop.
actions.public_tradeoff: High impact but 70% accurate.

actions.name: Dragon Dance
actions.explanation: Attempts to turn Ampharos into a fast special sweeper.
actions.public_tradeoff: Strong if safe; greedy into Ice/Recover pressure.

actions.name: Outrage
actions.explanation: Dragon STAB that locks Ampharos.
actions.public_tradeoff: Lock can be punished by Tyranitar or recovery pivots.

actions.name: Switch to Steelix
actions.explanation: Preserves Ampharos if Porygon2 pressure is too awkward.
actions.public_tradeoff: Defensive but loses initiative.

fixtures.training_focus: Kingdra chooses between Dragon Dance setup, immediate neutral damage, and pivoting around bulky Water recovery.

public_notes: Kingdra resists Water and is not obviously threatened by Surf.
Recover can erase weak attacks.
Dragon Dance can be correct if the incoming damage is low enough.

actions.name: Dragon Dance
actions.explanation: Sets up Kingdra when Starmie cannot punish enough.
actions.public_tradeoff: Strong setup window if Ice Beam risk is tolerable.

actions.name: Outrage
actions.explanation: Immediate Dragon damage that prevents free Recover tempo.
actions.public_tradeoff: Locks into a bulky Water matchup.

actions.name: Blizzard
actions.explanation: High-power coverage with shaky accuracy.
actions.public_tradeoff: Can miss and give Starmie recovery room.

actions.name: Surf
actions.explanation: Reliable but resisted Water damage.
actions.public_tradeoff: Too passive if Starmie can Recover.

actions.name: Switch to Ampharos
actions.explanation: Aligns Electric pressure into a Water type.
actions.public_tradeoff: Takes chip on entry and may reveal the plan.

fixtures.training_focus: Red's current Pikachu has ExtremeSpeed, not Quick Attack or Thunder; compare priority chip against the stronger Light Ball hit.

public_notes: Pikachu outspeeds Dragonite.
Light Ball doubles Pikachu special damage.
ExtremeSpeed priority is wasted when Pikachu already moves first.

actions.name: Thunderbolt
actions.explanation: Best reliable damage with Light Ball.
actions.public_tradeoff: Optimal first hit.

actions.name: ExtremeSpeed
actions.explanation: Priority chip when Pikachu does not need priority.
actions.public_tradeoff: Wastes the speed advantage.

actions.name: Surf
actions.explanation: Coverage that is resisted by Dragonite.
actions.public_tradeoff: Wrong coverage into this target.

actions.name: Switch to Blastoise
actions.explanation: Trades Pikachu tempo for a bulky pivot.
actions.public_tradeoff: No reason to give up the lead role yet.

fixtures.training_focus: Red's current Snorlax no longer has Amnesia or Self-Destruct; teach Curse versus direct Body Slam under Psychic/Recover pressure.

public_notes: Alakazam is faster and has revealed Recover.
Body Slam threatens damage plus paralysis.
Curse boosts the wrong defensive side into Psychic pressure but can snowball if Alakazam cannot break through.

actions.name: Body Slam
actions.explanation: Direct chip with paralysis chance into a faster Recover user.
actions.public_tradeoff: Keeps tempo and can enable later setup.

actions.name: Curse
actions.explanation: Atk+Def setup while Psychic pressure is special.
actions.public_tradeoff: Potentially greedy before paralysis or chip.

actions.name: Rest
actions.explanation: Full heal if Snorlax is truly losing the trade.
actions.public_tradeoff: Too early at high HP.

actions.name: Sleep Talk
actions.explanation: Only useful while asleep.
actions.public_tradeoff: Public fail if used awake.

fixtures.training_focus: Red's current Charizard has Flamethrower, Wing Attack, Sunny Day, and SolarBeam; teach when the sun package is too slow under Surf pressure.

public_notes: Starmie outspeeds Charizard.
Sunny Day plus SolarBeam is a two-turn plan from neutral weather.
Surf threatens Charizard 2x immediately.

actions.name: Switch to Pikachu
actions.explanation: Aligns Thunderbolt into Starmie.
actions.public_tradeoff: Best threat alignment if Red can afford the switch chip.

actions.name: Sunny Day
actions.explanation: Sets up SolarBeam but gives Starmie a Surf turn.
actions.public_tradeoff: Greedy two-turn plan under direct pressure.

actions.name: SolarBeam
actions.explanation: Super-effective but charges without sun.
actions.public_tradeoff: Likely interrupted by Surf.

actions.name: Wing Attack
actions.explanation: Neutral revealed hit.
actions.public_tradeoff: Lower pressure than switching.

actions.name: Flamethrower
actions.explanation: Reliable STAB but resisted by Water.
actions.public_tradeoff: Wrong damage lane into Starmie.

fixtures.training_focus: Current Lance Dragonite uses Earthquake, not Fire Blast or Thunder, to punish Steel/Dragon Steelix.

public_notes: Steelix is Steel/Dragon in this hack.
Earthquake is the clean public coverage hit.
Outrage is neutral because Steel resistance and Dragon weakness offset each other.

actions.name: Earthquake
actions.explanation: Best legal coverage into Steel/Dragon Steelix.
actions.public_tradeoff: Correct public type-chart answer.

actions.name: Outrage
actions.explanation: STAB damage that locks Dragonite.
actions.public_tradeoff: Acceptable in a 1v1, but worse than direct coverage.

actions.name: Dragon Dance
actions.explanation: Setup before attacking.
actions.public_tradeoff: Greedy into revealed Rock Slide / Steel pressure.

actions.name: Hyper Beam
actions.explanation: Big neutral hit with recharge liability.
actions.public_tradeoff: Bad unless it clearly ends the fight.

fixtures.training_focus: Mirror Coat Blastoise: teach when a visible special attacker makes the reactive trap a real line.

public_notes: Thunderbolt has been revealed, so Mirror Coat is legal public information.
Mirror Coat is only good if Blastoise is expected to survive the special hit.
Earthquake does not hit Zapdos.

actions.name: Mirror Coat
actions.explanation: Reflects the revealed special Electric attack if Blastoise survives.
actions.public_tradeoff: High-upside reactive trap, not hidden-move reading.

actions.name: Blizzard
actions.explanation: Direct super-effective Ice hit with shaky accuracy.
actions.public_tradeoff: Safer than guessing if survival is questionable.

actions.name: Surf
actions.explanation: Reliable resisted damage.
actions.public_tradeoff: Too passive into Zapdos.

actions.name: Earthquake
actions.explanation: Strong coverage that fails into Flying.
actions.public_tradeoff: Major bot tell if chosen.

actions.name: Switch to Snorlax
actions.explanation: Special sponge pivot into Electric pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Preserves Blastoise but gives up Mirror Coat payoff.

fixtures.training_focus: Mirror Coat negative example: do not click the reactive special trap into a physical Rock/Dark bruiser when Surf is obvious.

public_notes: Surf is super-effective into Tyranitar.
Mirror Coat only answers special damage and does nothing useful into Rock Slide or Earthquake.
Crunch is special in Gen 2, but Tyranitar has a clear physical punish profile.

actions.name: Surf
actions.explanation: Reliable super-effective STAB into Tyranitar.
actions.public_tradeoff: Simple correct pressure.

actions.name: Earthquake
actions.explanation: Physical coverage into Rock/Dark.
actions.public_tradeoff: Likely worse than Mystic Water Surf.

actions.name: Mirror Coat
actions.explanation: Reactive special trap with poor public target fit here.
actions.public_tradeoff: Too cute when Surf is obvious.

actions.name: Blizzard
actions.explanation: Coverage with lower reliability.
actions.public_tradeoff: Accuracy risk for no clear payoff.

fixtures.training_focus: Yanma is now on Lance's Dragon roster; compare legal sleep pressure against greedy Quiver Dance into Ice risk.

public_notes: Sleep Powder is legal and high-impact if it lands.
Quiver Dance can snowball but gives Lapras a public Ice window.
Giga Drain is useful but may not remove Lapras immediately.

actions.name: Sleep Powder
actions.explanation: Disables a bulky Ice-risk target before it can punish.
actions.public_tradeoff: Strong but accuracy-dependent.

actions.name: Quiver Dance
actions.explanation: Attempts to snowball Yanma through speed and special pressure.
actions.public_tradeoff: Greedy if Lapras has Ice coverage.

actions.name: Giga Drain
actions.explanation: Immediate super-effective sustain damage.
actions.public_tradeoff: Keeps tempo if sleep risk is not worth it.

actions.name: Switch to Kingdra
actions.explanation: Preserves Yanma and handles Water/Ice pressure better.
actions.public_tradeoff: Conservative champion pivot.

fixtures.training_focus: Turn a preserved but sleeping Snorlax into a concrete setup-window test: Curse is strong only because wake timing, hazards, and Rhydon's KO route have been tracked.

public_priors: Snorlax may wake soon but cannot act while asleep

public_notes: Snorlax was put to sleep by Lovely Kiss on turn 2 and is still asleep after returning late.
Rhydon has already removed Golem and can use the sleep window to change the KO math.
Curse costs speed, so the line must still account for Snorlax waking and acting before Rhydon.

actions.name: Curse
actions.explanation: Uses the sleep window to make Earthquake strong enough to trade through Snorlax.
actions.public_tradeoff: Excellent if the wake branch still leaves Rhydon favored; bad if wake timing or a hidden move flips the exchange.

actions.name: Earthquake
actions.explanation: Immediate damage into sleeping Snorlax.
actions.public_tradeoff: Keeps tempo but may fail to convert before Snorlax wakes.

actions.name: Rock Slide
actions.explanation: Useful for Zapdos or Moltres, but weak into the active Snorlax.
actions.public_tradeoff: Only makes sense on a strong Flying-type switch read.

actions.name: Switch to Zapdos
actions.explanation: Preserves Rhydon and keeps a final special route available.
actions.public_tradeoff: Gives the sleeping Snorlax a chance to wake without forcing progress.

fixtures.training_focus: Full-HP Rest is not a status cure in the local engine; the move fails before clearing status.

public_notes: Local Rest checks full HP before the status-cure path.
Paralysis does not make full-HP Rest legal.
Sleep Talk is also public-failing because Snorlax is not asleep.

actions.name: Body Slam
actions.explanation: Keeps pressure while Rest is mechanically unavailable.
actions.public_tradeoff: Does not cure paralysis, but at least advances the turn.

actions.name: Rest
actions.explanation: Would be a status cure below full HP.
actions.public_tradeoff: Fails at full HP in the local engine before curing status.

actions.name: Sleep Talk
actions.explanation: Only useful while asleep.
actions.public_tradeoff: Fails while awake.

actions.name: Curse
actions.explanation: Can build a route if Whirlwind does not erase the boost.
actions.public_tradeoff: Still risks donating a phazing turn.
